title: The Corporate Creep of Plex vs. The Jellyfin Dilemma
excerpt: Why Plex corporate creep restricts system freedom, how Jellyfin restores control and why Plexamp maybe it's true killer app
content_html:
Starting this week we're beginning a new series I'd like to call the Thursday Thinkpiece. Some things that have been noodling around in my brain for some time with some of it fit to print.

For over a decade, Plex was the main gateway into home media servers for tech enthusiasts, DIYers, and homelabbers alike. If you're unfamiliar with it, think of Plex as your own private, self-hosted Netflix or Spotify. You don't need an expensive server rack or a dedicated lab to run it: you can easily install the server software on an old spare laptop, an unused desktop PC, a simple NAS, or even an external hard drive plugged into a Raspberry Pi holding your personal movie, TV show, and music files. Plex then automatically organizes your libraries, pulls in poster artwork and movie details, and streams your content seamlessly across a huge range of devices. Whether on LG webOS, Samsung Tizen, Apple TV, Roku, Fire TV, iOS, Android, or game consoles, Plex made it insanely easy for anyone in the house to log in and watch or listen to their content instantly.

My own media streaming journey actually started way back in late 2002 / 2003 with the early days of XBMC (Xbox Media Center) running on the original modded Xbox console. Back then, managing a home media center meant spending endless hours manually scraping artwork, tweaking NFO files, and messing around with XML settings. But as life got busier, I simply didn't have the time to go deep into the metadata side of things anymore. That's when I switched to Plex, because it just worked straight out of the box without a lot of messing around.

Having relied on Plex for years after making that transition from XBMC, I bought a Lifetime Plex Pass for $74.99 USD (~£54 GBP) during a sale in February 2021, right in the middle of the pandemic lockdowns. Being stuck at home meant spending a lot more time refining my at-home services, and that era became the turning point where I truly threw myself into self-hosting and expanded my setup. Buying the lifetime pass felt right: it was a way of giving back to a project that kept my household entertainment running smoothly after years of free use, while also unlocking Plex Pass features like hardware-accelerated GPU transcoding, mobile sync, and intro skipping.

But over the last few years, the atmosphere surrounding Plex has shifted. The software has experienced a slow shift toward corporate creep (or "enshittification"), where a company slowly prioritizes corporate deals and advertising over its original core users.

As a long-time self-hoster who values complete control over my hardware and setup, watching this change has been an eye-opening lesson in tech ownership. It shows why it may be time to move to Jellyfin for true system freedom, while also examining the open-source reality of what it takes to support community alternatives long-term.

Plex’s core business model has pivoted. Originally, they sold server software via the Plex Pass. Today, Plex’s main focus is becoming a centralized, ad-supported free streaming service (competing with PlutoTV or Tubi) backed by major studio licensing deals.

To be fair, sponsored content and Free Ad-supported Streaming TV (FAST) channels are great for casual viewers who don't host their own media. Having free movies and live channels available out-of-the-box adds genuine convenience for millions of users. However, these commercial content deals have subtly shifted Plex's focus away from its original mission: serving your own personal media seamlessly.

Open the default Plex app on a new device today, and your personal server’s libraries are buried. Instead, the home screen's populated by Plex’s own free ad-supported movies, sponsored TV channels, and commercial rows. While you can hide these in the settings, the fact that a self-hosted server defaults to pushing external ad-supported media shows where Plex’s engineering priorities and corporate revenue now lie.

Studio Pressure & Corporate Alignment: Crackdowns and Remote Watch Passes

As Plex partnered with major Hollywood studios to license free streaming content, corporate pressure mounted to clean up its platform and align with commercial streaming standards:

  • The Commercial Host Crackdown: Plex began aggressively targeting accounts selling access to commercial Plex servers. This escalated into blanket bans on major cloud hosting providers (like banning entire Hetzner and OVH server IP ranges) and purging accounts tied to paid library sharing. While stopping illegal server sales makes sense legally, banning entire data center IP ranges also hurt regular homelabbers who simply hosted their own private servers off-site.
  • Subscription Gating & Remote Watch Passes: To satisfy content partners and monetize out-of-home viewers, Plex introduced new subscription friction like Remote Watch Passes (requiring remote viewers or server owners to pay for out-of-home playback) while tightening how Lifetime Plex Pass benefits apply across shared accounts. What was once seamless, open sharing among family has increasingly been restructured into gated corporate tiers.

2. The Centralized Authentication Trap

From a system control standpoint, Plex has a single point of failure that breaks the fundamental promise of owning your server: Centralized Authentication.

When you log in to your Plex server, you don't authenticate locally against your host database. Instead, your app redirects to Plex.tv to log in via their central servers, which then sends a key back to unlock access to your local hardware.

  • The Outage Risk: Recurring authentication outages tracked on Status.Plex.tv, as well as major incidents covered by high-authority tech publications like BleepingComputer and How-To Geek, have showed just how risky this setup is. When Plex's cloud infrastructure experiences downtime or DNS disruptions, thousands of users worldwide (including paying Plex Pass subscribers) find themselves locked out of their own local servers sitting right in their living rooms, unable to stream local files unless they've already set up offline network settings in advance.
  • The Data Collection Concern: Every time a user opens your Plex app, they're talking to Plex's central servers. Plex collects data on when you watch, what device you stream on, and what items exist in your watchlists.

Compare this to Jellyfin: authentication's 100% local. Your Jellyfin server is an independent, self-contained unit. If the internet connection drops or central servers fail, your local login and local streaming remain completely unaffected. You're retaining total freedom over your own hardware.

3. Scope Creep and Feature Churn: Improved Downloads vs. Deprecated Features

Plex's feature development roadmap has been a mixed bag of great technical fixes alongside frustrating feature removals:

  • The Good: Mobile Downloads Finally Work: To Plex's credit, core features like Mobile Downloads (Sync), which were notoriously buggy and unreliable for years, have recently seen dedicated engineering focus and now work vastly better and more reliably across iOS and Android devices.
  • The Bad: Deprecated Features Like Watch Together: Conversely, users frequently face the frustration of beloved features being pulled out from under them. Features like Watch Together, which became an essential lifeline during the pandemic for synchronized remote viewing with friends and family, have been phased out or removed, leaving long-time users disappointed when tools they relied on disappear.
  • Social Additions: Meanwhile, features like Plex Discover (which directs users to commercial streaming services) and Social Activity Feeds pull engineering attention toward engagement metrics and data collection, further drifting away from the core mission of managing personal local media.

4. The Open-Source Response: Why Jellyfin Wins (And My Recommendation for Newcomers)

This corporate shift is exactly why Jellyfin (forked from Emby when Emby went closed-source in 2018) won the hearts of the homelab community.

I've spun up Jellyfin in my own homelab alongside Plex to test its performance, client apps, and local reliability. Jellyfin is built on a simple, refreshing premise: It's your server, your media, and your data.

  • No Central Account: You don't register an account with a central server. User accounts are created locally on your hardware.
  • Zero Tracking: There aren't any corporate tracking metrics, ad rows, or forced discoverability features.
  • Free Native Hardware Transcoding: Premium features that Plex charges for (like GPU hardware transcoding for smooth playback) are completely free in Jellyfin.

The Client Ecosystem Trade-Off: Corporate Standard vs. Scattered Community Apps

A realistic comparison between the two platforms also comes down to client availability and app stability:

  • Plex's Unbeatable Reach: Because Plex operates as a commercial company with dedicated app development teams, they maintain rock-solid native apps across virtually every Smart TV app store worldwide (LG webOS, Samsung Tizen, Apple TV, Roku, Fire TV, PlayStation, Xbox). Non-technical family members can download the app and start watching instantly without tinkering.
  • Jellyfin's Scattered Client Reality: Jellyfin has client apps (such as Android TV, Swiftfin for iOS/Apple TV, Roku, and web interfaces), but because there isn't a single company managing them, client development is split across separate volunteer projects. As a result, apps aren't always rock-solid or feature-complete on every screen. A video file that plays smoothly on Android TV might crash on a Smart TV app wrapper, and native apps aren't always easily available on older TV app stores.

My Advice for Newcomers: If you're getting into self-hosting today and building your first home server, I strongly suggest starting with Jellyfin. You avoid corporate bloat, mandatory cloud logins, and paywalled features right out of the box; just keep in mind which TV devices your household uses.

5. The Open-Source Reality Check: Burnout, Governance, and the July 2026 Leadership Shakeup

While Jellyfin represents the gold standard for true system control and personal freedom, open source isn't immune to crises of its own.

In July 2026, the Jellyfin project experienced a massive leadership transition when several core maintainers, including Project Leader Joshua Boniface, Core Team Member Anthony Lavado, and Co-founder Andrew Rabert, announced their departures within days of each other.

This high-profile exit highlighted a growing challenge across open-source software:

  1. Maintainer Burnout: Supporting millions of users as an unpaid side project takes a heavy mental toll.
  2. Unrealistic Expectations: As homelab setups grow in popularity, volunteer developers increasingly face demanding end-user expectations, with users expecting unpaid volunteers to act like 24/7 paid support teams.
  3. The AI-Generated PR Deluge: Modern maintainers face a flood of low-quality, AI-generated code submissions that clutter issue trackers, forcing maintainers to spend hours auditing unverified code instead of building features.

This news is an important reality check for homelabbers. When proprietary platforms like Plex restrict system freedom, we turn to open-source alternatives. But open source only survives if the community actively supports it.

6. Early Adopter Reality: Why I Still Use Plex (And Why I Run Jellyfin in Parallel)

Looking at both sides of the coin reveals an important truth: self-hosting isn't an all-or-nothing choice.

As an early adopter who built my setup around Plex over nearly a decade, I still use Plex every single day. For an existing setup that's running smoothly, the classic homelab rule applies: if it ain't broke, don't fix it.

I've set up Jellyfin myself alongside Plex in my homelab, and I run both servers in parallel. I follow Jellyfin's development closely, and one day, when the client app ecosystem and project maturity reach the right point, I may very well flick the switch and migrate 100%. But full migration isn't for me just yet; Plex remains my primary daily driver.

This sentiment shift isn't unique to my homelab either. Even Linus Sebastian from Linus Tech Tips (LTT), historically one of Plex's biggest supporters online, slowly started complaining more about Plex's trajectory. On The WAN Show and LTT videos, Linus voiced growing frustration over cloud authentication lockouts, corporate pivots, and software bloat. Eventually, Linus Tech Tips went on to produce dedicated video content showcasing open-source alternatives like Jellyfin, showing how widespread the desire for self-hosted alternatives has become across tech culture.

When I bought my Lifetime Plex Pass back in February 2021 for $74.99 (~£54 GBP), the math made complete sense. Over the last 5.5 years (roughly 66 months of continuous daily use), that initial purchase works out to just ~£9.80 per year (or roughly 82p / $1.13 per month).

And chief among the tools justifying that purchase is Plexamp.

Plexamp is an absolute masterpiece of audio engineering. By self-hosting my music collection and streaming it via Plexamp, whether listening on mobile devices, home audio systems, or in the car via Android Auto and Apple CarPlay, I was able to completely stop paying for monthly subscriptions to Spotify and Amazon Music.

At ~£10.99/month ($11.99/month) for a commercial music streaming subscription, Plexamp paid for my Lifetime Pass within five months. Over 5.5 years, it's saved me well over £700 ($900) in recurring music streaming bills.

To Plex's credit, they've been remarkably good at adding value to the Lifetime Pass over the years, rolling out tools like Plexamp, intro skipping, hardware transcoding, and mobile downloads without asking early pass holders for another penny. Even if Plex eventually alters its terms down the road, I've undoubtedly gotten my money's worth out of that $75 purchase, and many other long-time users feel the exact same way.

But it raises a bigger question for the tech industry: Is a one-time lifetime license model actually sustainable long-term?

History shows how rare it is for tech companies to honor lifetime promises forever as cloud infrastructure costs and investor pressures grow. Major tech media coverage highlights how lifetime deals have been altered or revoked across the industry before:

  • TiVo: Sold popular "Lifetime Service" passes for DVRs, but as reported by The Verge, TiVo eventually phased out hardware lines, shut down guide data servers, and left legacy devices non-functional.
  • SiriusXM: Sold $500 "Lifetime" satellite radio subscriptions, but restricted device transfers and added hidden fees, resulting in a major nationwide class-action lawsuit detailed by Courthouse News.
  • LogMeIn: Famously eliminated its popular "free for life" remote access tier with just seven days' notice to force users onto paid plans, as covered by TechCrunch.
  • Evernote & Malwarebytes: Malwarebytes grandfathered early lifetime keys before shifting strictly to annual plans, while Evernote stripped features out of legacy accounts to push users into new paid tiers, as reported by The Verge.

The MRR Trap: Can Chasing Subscriptions Go On Forever?

In today's software landscape, both solo developers and massive tech corporations are hyper-focused on securing Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR). To chase predictable recurring cash flow, companies increasingly rely on sneaky tactics: locking existing local features behind paywalls, adding unexpected subscription gates, and quietly changing terms of service to turn one-time buyers into permanent renters.

It raises a critical question for both creators and consumers: Can this endless pursuit of MRR through sneaky tactics really go on forever? When software transitions from serving users to extracting monthly rent for hardware sitting in your own living room, it creates a deep trust deficit that drives users straight to open-source alternatives.

When a company realizes one-time lifetime payments can't fund perpetual development and server hosting, they inevitably pivot toward ad networks, locked feature tiers, or price hikes, which is precisely what we're watching unfold with Plex today.

The Problem Today: Huge Price Increases & Eroding Value

However, what made financial sense in 2021 looks completely unrecognizable today. Historically, prospective homelabbers knew they could rely on Plex's regular annual sales events, such as Black Friday, Cyber Monday, February anniversary sales, Pro Days, or targeted email promo codes offering lifetime passes for $75 to $90.

For years, standard community advice for newcomers was simple: "Just hold off and wait for the Black Friday sale."

Many users held off for months or years expecting those familiar discounts to return. But people were caught completely off-guard when Plex silently ended those sales events and instead hit the community with massive price hikes, covered across major tech media outlets like AppleInsider and official Plex Pass documentation:

  • The Lifetime Pass: Historically $119.99 USD (and $75–$90 during sales), Plex first increased it to $249.99, before skyrocketing the Lifetime Plex Pass to an eye-watering $749.99 USD (~£595+ GBP) in July 2026.
  • New 5-Year Tier: Alongside the $750 lifetime price tag, Plex introduced a 5-Year Pass at $249.99 USD, effectively shifting what was once a true lifetime purchase into an expensive multi-year subscription cycle.
  • The Monthly Alternative: Granted, you don't have to pay $750 up-front, as Plex still offers a monthly subscription at $6.99 / £6.99 per month.

But that brings us to a fundamental question for self-hosters: Should you really have to pay a recurring monthly rent to a third-party company just to access and unlock features (like GPU hardware transcoding) on hardware you bought and own in your own home?

Asking a new user to pay $750 up-front (or shell out $6.99 every single month indefinitely) for software that increasingly buries your personal media below ad-supported FAST channels, pushes commercial studio content, and locks your local server out during cloud authentication outages feels really hard to justify. When Jellyfin provides native GPU hardware transcoding, local multi-user auth, and full media streaming for $0, the reason to buy into Plex today just isn't there anymore for anyone starting fresh.

The Pragmatic Verdict: Complete System Freedom & Active Stewardship

The debate between Plex and Jellyfin isn't about rigid tech dogma; it's about practical utility and freedom.

  • If you're starting out today: Go with Jellyfin. It gives you total control over your hardware without cloud gatekeeping or corporate ad clutter.
  • If you're a long-time Plex Pass user: There's zero shame in keeping Plex as your daily driver while running Jellyfin alongside it in your home setup. Running both in parallel lets you enjoy tools like Plexamp today while positioning your infrastructure to flick the switch over completely when Jellyfin reaches full client maturity.

However, we can't treat open-source projects as "free enterprise software." If we want alternatives like Jellyfin to thrive alongside commercial tools, tech enthusiasts, DIYers and homelabbers must actively contribute back, whether through financial sponsorship, documentation improvements, code reviews, or setting healthy community boundaries. If you enjoy using Jellyfin or want to help the maintainers out, consider donating to the Jellyfin project to help support ongoing development and infrastructure.

At the end of the day, I really like Plex, and to be clear, Plex isn't evil. They're walking a tough corporate tightrope, trying to serve millions of different users while remaining as legally compliant as possible with major Hollywood studios and copyright regulations.

Managing a commercial business at that scale is no easy feat, and Plex may very well surprise us all down the road as they continue evolving. I've used Plex for a decade, and I'll continue using it as my primary daily driver alongside Jellyfin.

But open-source projects like Jellyfin still deserve their flowers. Having a robust, community-driven alternative is vital for healthy competition overall: keeping commercial platforms honest, preventing total vendor lock-in and guaranteeing that tech enthusiasts always have a path to complete system freedom.

Don't forget to explore the rest of our website as we build out more content. Stay tuned for more tutorials, tips, and tricks to help you make tech work for you.

If you want to stay up-to-date with regular updates, make sure to subscribe to our free mailing list.

When software transitions from serving users to extracting monthly rent for hardware sitting in your own living room, it creates a deep trust deficit that drives users straight to open-source alternatives. When a company realizes one-time lifetime payments can't fund perpetual development and server hosting, they inevitably pivot toward ad networks, locked feature tiers, or price hikes, which is precisely what we're watching unfold with Plex today. The Problem Today: Huge Price Increases & Eroding Value However, what made financial sense in 2021 looks completely unrecognizable today. Historically, prospective homelabbers knew they could rely on Plex's regular annual sales events, such as Black Friday, Cyber Monday, February anniversary sales, Pro Days, or targeted email promo codes offering lifetime passes for $75 to $90. For years, standard community advice for newcomers was simple: "Just hold off and wait for the Black Friday sale." Many users held off for months or years expecting those familiar discounts to return. But people were caught completely off-guard when Plex silently ended those sales events and instead hit the community with massive price hikes, covered across major tech media outlets like AppleInsider and official Plex Pass documentation: The Lifetime Pass: Historically $119.99 USD (and $75–$90 during sales), Plex first increased it to $249.99, before skyrocketing the Lifetime Plex Pass to an eye-watering $749.99 USD (~£595+ GBP) in July 2026. New 5-Year Tier: Alongside the $750 lifetime price tag, Plex introduced a 5-Year Pass at $249.99 USD, effectively shifting what was once a true lifetime purchase into an expensive multi-year subscription cycle. The Monthly Alternative: Granted, you don't have to pay $750 up-front, as Plex still offers a monthly subscription at $6.99 / £6.99 per month. But that brings us to a fundamental question for self-hosters: Should you really have to pay a recurring monthly rent to a third-party company just to access and unlock features (like GPU hardware transcoding) on hardware you bought and own in your own home? Asking a new user to pay $750 up-front (or shell out $6.99 every single month indefinitely) for software that increasingly buries your personal media below ad-supported FAST channels, pushes commercial studio content, and locks your local server out during cloud authentication outages feels really hard to justify. When Jellyfin provides native GPU hardware transcoding, local multi-user auth, and full media streaming for $0, the reason to buy into Plex today just isn't there anymore for anyone starting fresh. The Pragmatic Verdict: Complete System Freedom & Active Stewardship The debate between Plex and Jellyfin isn't about rigid tech dogma; it's about practical utility and freedom. If you're starting out today: Go with Jellyfin. It gives you total control over your hardware without cloud gatekeeping or corporate ad clutter. If you're a long-time Plex Pass user: There's zero shame in keeping Plex as your daily driver while running Jellyfin alongside it in your home setup. Running both in parallel lets you enjoy tools like Plexamp today while positioning your infrastructure to flick the switch over completely when Jellyfin reaches full client maturity. However, we can't treat open-source projects as "free enterprise software." If we want alternatives like Jellyfin to thrive alongside commercial tools, tech enthusiasts, DIYers and homelabbers must actively contribute back, whether through financial sponsorship, documentation improvements, code reviews, or setting healthy community boundaries. If you enjoy using Jellyfin or want to help the maintainers out, consider donating to the Jellyfin project to help support ongoing development and infrastructure. At the end of the day, I really like Plex, and to be clear, Plex isn't evil. They're walking a tough corporate tightrope, trying to serve millions of different users while remaining as legally compliant as possible with major Hollywood studios and copyright regulations. Managing a commercial business at that scale is no easy feat, and Plex may very well surprise us all down the road as they continue evolving. I've used Plex for a decade, and I'll continue using it as my primary daily driver alongside Jellyfin. But open-source projects like Jellyfin still deserve their flowers. Having a robust, community-driven alternative is vital for healthy competition overall: keeping commercial platforms honest, preventing total vendor lock-in and guaranteeing that tech enthusiasts always have a path to complete system freedom. Don't forget to explore the rest of our website as we build out more content. Stay tuned for more tutorials, tips, and tricks to help you make tech work for you.If you want to stay up-to-date with regular updates, make sure to subscribe to our free mailing list.

blocks.html_en: For over a decade, Plex was the main gateway into home media servers for tech enthusiasts, DIYers, and homelabbers alike. If you're unfamiliar with it, think of Plex as your own private, self-hosted Netflix or Spotify. You don't need an expensive server rack or a dedicated lab to run it: you can easily install the server software on an old spare laptop, an unused desktop PC, a simple NAS, or even an external hard drive plugged into a Raspberry Pi holding your personal movie, TV show, and music files. Plex then automatically organizes your libraries, pulls in poster artwork and movie details, and streams your content seamlessly across a huge range of devices. Whether on LG webOS, Samsung Tizen, Apple TV, Roku, Fire TV, iOS, Android, or game consoles, Plex made it insanely easy for anyone in the house to log in and watch or listen to their content instantly.

blocks.html_en: My own media streaming journey actually started way back in late 2002 / 2003 with the early days of XBMC (Xbox Media Center) running on the original modded Xbox console. Back then, managing a home media center meant spending endless hours manually scraping artwork, tweaking NFO files, and messing around with XML settings. But as life got busier, I simply didn't have the time to go deep into the metadata side of things anymore. That's when I switched to Plex, because it just worked straight out of the box without a lot of messing around.

sentences.en: That's when I switched to Plex, because it just worked straight out of the box without a lot of messing around.

blocks.html_en: Having relied on Plex for years after making that transition from XBMC, I bought a Lifetime Plex Pass for $74.99 USD (~£54 GBP) during a sale in February 2021, right in the middle of the pandemic lockdowns. Being stuck at home meant spending a lot more time refining my at-home services, and that era became the turning point where I truly threw myself into self-hosting and expanded my setup. Buying the lifetime pass felt right: it was a way of giving back to a project that kept my household entertainment running smoothly after years of free use, while also unlocking Plex Pass features like hardware-accelerated GPU transcoding, mobile sync, and intro skipping.

sentences.en: Buying the lifetime pass felt right: it was a way of giving back to a project that kept my household entertainment running smoothly after years of free use, while also unlocking Plex Pass features like hardware-accelerated GPU transcoding, mobile sync, and intro skipping.
sentences.zh: 购买终身通行证感觉很正确：这是回馈项目的一种方式，该项目让我的家庭娱乐在多年免费使用后保持平稳运行，同时还解锁了 Plex Pass 功能，如硬件加速 GPU 转码、移动同步和介绍跳过。

blocks.html_en: But over the last few years, the atmosphere surrounding Plex has shifted. The software has experienced a slow shift toward corporate creep (or "enshittification"), where a company slowly prioritizes corporate deals and advertising over its original core users.

sentences.en: But over the last few years, the atmosphere surrounding Plex has shifted.
sentences.zh: 但在过去几年中，围绕 Plex 的气氛发生了变化。

sentences.en: The software has experienced a slow shift toward corporate creep (or "enshittification"), where a company slowly prioritizes corporate deals and advertising over its original core users.
sentences.zh: 该软件经历了向企业蠕变（或“enshittification”）的缓慢转变，即公司慢慢地将企业交易和广告置于其原始核心用户之上。

blocks.html_en: As a long-time self-hoster who values complete control over my hardware and setup, watching this change has been an eye-opening lesson in tech ownership. It shows why it may be time to move to Jellyfin for true system freedom, while also examining the open-source reality of what it takes to support community alternatives long-term.

blocks.html_en: Plex’s core business model has pivoted. Originally, they sold server software via the Plex Pass. Today, Plex’s main focus is becoming a centralized, ad-supported free streaming service (competing with PlutoTV or Tubi) backed by major studio licensing deals.

blocks.html_en: To be fair, sponsored content and Free Ad-supported Streaming TV (FAST) channels are great for casual viewers who don't host their own media. Having free movies and live channels available out-of-the-box adds genuine convenience for millions of users. However, these commercial content deals have subtly shifted Plex's focus away from its original mission: serving your own personal media seamlessly.

blocks.html_en: Open the default Plex app on a new device today, and your personal server’s libraries are buried. Instead, the home screen's populated by Plex’s own free ad-supported movies, sponsored TV channels, and commercial rows. While you can hide these in the settings, the fact that a self-hosted server defaults to pushing external ad-supported media shows where Plex’s engineering priorities and corporate revenue now lie.

blocks.html_en: Studio Pressure & Corporate Alignment: Crackdowns and Remote Watch Passes

sentences.en: Studio Pressure & Corporate Alignment: Crackdowns and Remote Watch Passes
sentences.zh: 工作室压力和公司联盟：镇压和远程值守通行证

blocks.html_en: As Plex partnered with major Hollywood studios to license free streaming content, corporate pressure mounted to clean up its platform and align with commercial streaming standards:

sentences.en: As Plex partnered with major Hollywood studios to license free streaming content, corporate pressure mounted to clean up its platform and align with commercial streaming standards:
sentences.zh: 随着 Plex 与主要好莱坞电影公司合作获得免费流媒体内容许可，企业面临着清理其平台并符合商业流媒体标准的压力：

blocks.html_en: • The Commercial Host Crackdown: Plex began aggressively targeting accounts selling access to commercial Plex servers. This escalated into blanket bans on major cloud hosting providers (like banning entire Hetzner and OVH server IP ranges) and purging accounts tied to paid library sharing. While stopping illegal server sales makes sense legally, banning entire data center IP ranges also hurt regular homelabbers who simply hosted their own private servers off-site.

sentences.en: The Commercial Host Crackdown: Plex began aggressively targeting accounts selling access to commercial Plex servers.
sentences.zh: 商业主机打击：Plex 开始积极针对出售商业 Plex 服务器访问权限的帐户。

sentences.en: This escalated into blanket bans on major cloud hosting providers (like banning entire Hetzner and OVH server IP ranges) and purging accounts tied to paid library sharing.
sentences.zh: 这升级为对主要云托管提供商的全面禁令（例如禁止整个 Hetzner 和 OVH 服务器 IP 范围）并清除与付费图书馆共享相关的帐户。

sentences.en: While stopping illegal server sales makes sense legally, banning entire data center IP ranges also hurt regular homelabbers who simply hosted their own private servers off-site.
sentences.zh: 虽然停止非法服务器销售在法律上是有意义的，但禁止整个数据中心 IP 范围也会伤害那些只是在场外托管自己的私人服务器的普通家庭劳动者。

blocks.html_en: • Subscription Gating & Remote Watch Passes: To satisfy content partners and monetize out-of-home viewers, Plex introduced new subscription friction like Remote Watch Passes (requiring remote viewers or server owners to pay for out-of-home playback) while tightening how Lifetime Plex Pass benefits apply across shared accounts. What was once seamless, open sharing among family has increasingly been restructured into gated corporate tiers.

sentences.en: Subscription Gating & Remote Watch Passes: To satisfy content partners and monetize out-of-home viewers, Plex introduced new subscription friction like Remote Watch Passes (requiring remote viewers or server owners to pay for out-of-home playback) while tightening how Lifetime Plex Pass benefits apply across shared accounts.
sentences.zh: 订阅门控和远程观看通行证：为了满足内容合作伙伴的需求并通过户外观看者获利，Plex 引入了新的订阅摩擦，例如远程观看通行证（要求远程观看者或服务器所有者为户外播放付费），同时加强了终身 Plex Pass 权益在共享帐户中的应用方式。

sentences.en: What was once seamless, open sharing among family has increasingly been restructured into gated corporate tiers.
sentences.zh: 家庭之间曾经无缝、开放的共享已逐渐被重组为封闭的企业层级。

blocks.html_en: 2. The Centralized Authentication Trap

sentences.en: The Centralized Authentication Trap
sentences.zh: 集中式身份验证陷阱

blocks.html_en: From a system control standpoint, Plex has a single point of failure that breaks the fundamental promise of owning your server: Centralized Authentication.

sentences.en: From a system control standpoint, Plex has a single point of failure that breaks the fundamental promise of owning your server: Centralized Authentication.
sentences.zh: 从系统控制的角度来看，Plex 存在一个单点故障，它破坏了拥有服务器的基本承诺：集中身份验证。

blocks.html_en: When you log in to your Plex server, you don't authenticate locally against your host database. Instead, your app redirects to Plex.tv to log in via their central servers, which then sends a key back to unlock access to your local hardware.

sentences.en: When you log in to your Plex server, you don't authenticate locally against your host database.
sentences.zh: 当您登录 Plex 服务器时，您不会根据主机数据库进行本地身份验证。

sentences.en: Instead, your app redirects to Plex.tv to log in via their central servers, which then sends a key back to unlock access to your local hardware.
sentences.zh: 相反，您的应用程序会重定向到 Plex.tv 以通过其中央服务器登录，然后服务器会发回密钥以解锁对本地硬件的访问。

blocks.html_en: • The Outage Risk: Recurring authentication outages tracked on Status.Plex.tv, as well as major incidents covered by high-authority tech publications like BleepingComputer and How-To Geek, have showed just how risky this setup is. When Plex's cloud infrastructure experiences downtime or DNS disruptions, thousands of users worldwide (including paying Plex Pass subscribers) find themselves locked out of their own local servers sitting right in their living rooms, unable to stream local files unless they've already set up offline network settings in advance.

sentences.en: The Outage Risk: Recurring authentication outages tracked on Status.Plex.tv, as well as major incidents covered by high-authority tech publications like BleepingComputer and How-To Geek, have showed just how risky this setup is.
sentences.zh: 中断风险：Status.Plex.tv 上跟踪的反复出现的身份验证中断，以及 BleepingComputer 和 How-To Geek 等高权威技术出版物报道的重大事件，都表明了这种设置的风险有多大。

sentences.en: When Plex's cloud infrastructure experiences downtime or DNS disruptions, thousands of users worldwide (including paying Plex Pass subscribers) find themselves locked out of their own local servers sitting right in their living rooms, unable to stream local files unless they've already set up offline network settings in advance.
sentences.zh: 当 Plex 的云基础设施出现停机或 DNS 中断时，全球数以千计的用户（包括付费 Plex Pass 订阅者）发现自己被锁在客厅里的本地服务器之外，无法传输本地文件，除非他们已经提前设置了离线网络设置。

blocks.html_en: • The Data Collection Concern: Every time a user opens your Plex app, they're talking to Plex's central servers. Plex collects data on when you watch, what device you stream on, and what items exist in your watchlists.

sentences.en: The Data Collection Concern: Every time a user opens your Plex app, they're talking to Plex's central servers.
sentences.zh: 数据收集问题：每次用户打开您的 Plex 应用程序时，他们都会与 Plex 的中央服务器进行通信。

sentences.en: Plex collects data on when you watch, what device you stream on, and what items exist in your watchlists.
sentences.zh: Plex 收集有关您观看的时间、您使用的设备以及您的观看列表中存在哪些项目的数据。

blocks.html_en: Compare this to Jellyfin: authentication's 100% local. Your Jellyfin server is an independent, self-contained unit. If the internet connection drops or central servers fail, your local login and local streaming remain completely unaffected. You're retaining total freedom over your own hardware.

sentences.en: Compare this to Jellyfin: authentication's 100% local.
sentences.zh: 将此与 Jellyfin 进行比较：身份验证 100% 本地。

sentences.en: Your Jellyfin server is an independent, self-contained unit.
sentences.zh: 您的 Jellyfin 服务器是一个独立、独立的单元。

sentences.en: If the internet connection drops or central servers fail, your local login and local streaming remain completely unaffected.
sentences.zh: 如果互联网连接中断或中央服务器发生故障，您的本地登录和本地流媒体完全不受影响。

sentences.en: You're retaining total freedom over your own hardware.
sentences.zh: 您对自己的硬件保留完全的自由。

blocks.html_en: 3. Scope Creep and Feature Churn: Improved Downloads vs. Deprecated Features

sentences.en: Scope Creep and Feature Churn: Improved Downloads vs. Deprecated Features
sentences.zh: 范围蔓延和功能流失：改进的下载与弃用的功能

blocks.html_en: Plex's feature development roadmap has been a mixed bag of great technical fixes alongside frustrating feature removals:

sentences.en: Plex's feature development roadmap has been a mixed bag of great technical fixes alongside frustrating feature removals:
sentences.zh: Plex 的功能开发路线图既有出色的技术修复，也有令人沮丧的功能删除：

blocks.html_en: • The Good: Mobile Downloads Finally Work: To Plex's credit, core features like Mobile Downloads (Sync), which were notoriously buggy and unreliable for years, have recently seen dedicated engineering focus and now work vastly better and more reliably across iOS and Android devices.

sentences.en: The Good: Mobile Downloads Finally Work: To Plex's credit, core features like Mobile Downloads (Sync), which were notoriously buggy and unreliable for years, have recently seen dedicated engineering focus and now work vastly better and more reliably across iOS and Android devices.
sentences.zh: 优点：移动下载终于可以工作了：值得赞扬的是，移动下载（同步）等核心功能多年来一直以错误和不可靠而闻名，但最近得到了专门的工程关注，现在在 iOS 和 Android 设备上工作得更好、更可靠。

blocks.html_en: • The Bad: Deprecated Features Like Watch Together: Conversely, users frequently face the frustration of beloved features being pulled out from under them. Features like Watch Together, which became an essential lifeline during the pandemic for synchronized remote viewing with friends and family, have been phased out or removed, leaving long-time users disappointed when tools they relied on disappear.

sentences.en: The Bad: Deprecated Features Like Watch Together: Conversely, users frequently face the frustration of beloved features being pulled out from under them.
sentences.zh: 坏处：被弃用的功能（例如“一起观看”）：相反，用户经常会因心爱的功能被从其下删除而感到沮丧。

sentences.en: Features like Watch Together, which became an essential lifeline during the pandemic for synchronized remote viewing with friends and family, have been phased out or removed, leaving long-time users disappointed when tools they relied on disappear.
sentences.zh: 像“一起观看”这样的功能，在大流行期间成为与朋友和家人同步远程观看的重要生命线，但已被逐步淘汰或删除，让长期用户在他们依赖的工具消失时感到失望。

blocks.html_en: • Social Additions: Meanwhile, features like Plex Discover (which directs users to commercial streaming services) and Social Activity Feeds pull engineering attention toward engagement metrics and data collection, further drifting away from the core mission of managing personal local media.

sentences.en: Social Additions: Meanwhile, features like Plex Discover (which directs users to commercial streaming services) and Social Activity Feeds pull engineering attention toward engagement metrics and data collection, further drifting away from the core mission of managing personal local media.
sentences.zh: 社交附加功能：与此同时，Plex Discover（将用户引导至商业流媒体服务）和社交活动源等功能将工程人员的注意力吸引到参与度指标和数据收集上，进一步偏离了管理个人本地媒体的核心使命。

blocks.html_en: 4. The Open-Source Response: Why Jellyfin Wins (And My Recommendation for Newcomers)

sentences.en: The Open-Source Response: Why Jellyfin Wins (And My Recommendation for Newcomers)
sentences.zh: 开源回应：为什么 Jellyfin 获胜（以及我对新人的建议）

blocks.html_en: This corporate shift is exactly why Jellyfin (forked from Emby when Emby went closed-source in 2018) won the hearts of the homelab community.

sentences.en: This corporate shift is exactly why Jellyfin (forked from Emby when Emby went closed-source in 2018) won the hearts of the homelab community.
sentences.zh: 这种企业转变正是 Jellyfin（2018 年 Emby 闭源时从 Emby 分叉出来）赢得家庭实验室社区青睐的原因。

blocks.html_en: I've spun up Jellyfin in my own homelab alongside Plex to test its performance, client apps, and local reliability. Jellyfin is built on a simple, refreshing premise: It's your server, your media, and your data.

sentences.en: I've spun up Jellyfin in my own homelab alongside Plex to test its performance, client apps, and local reliability.
sentences.zh: 我在自己的家庭实验室中与 Plex 一起启动了 Jellyfin，以测试其性能、客户端应用程序和本地可靠性。

sentences.en: Jellyfin is built on a simple, refreshing premise: It's your server, your media, and your data.
sentences.zh: Jellyfin 建立在一个简单、令人耳目一新的前提之上：它是您的服务器、您的媒体和您的数据。

blocks.html_en: • No Central Account: You don't register an account with a central server. User accounts are created locally on your hardware.

sentences.en: No Central Account: You don't register an account with a central server.
sentences.zh: 无中央帐户：您不向中央服务器注册帐户。

sentences.en: User accounts are created locally on your hardware.
sentences.zh: 用户帐户是在您的硬件上本地创建的。

blocks.html_en: • Zero Tracking: There aren't any corporate tracking metrics, ad rows, or forced discoverability features.

sentences.en: Zero Tracking: There aren't any corporate tracking metrics, ad rows, or forced discoverability features.
sentences.zh: 零跟踪：没有任何公司跟踪指标、广告行或强制发现功能。

blocks.html_en: • Free Native Hardware Transcoding: Premium features that Plex charges for (like GPU hardware transcoding for smooth playback) are completely free in Jellyfin.

sentences.en: Free Native Hardware Transcoding: Premium features that Plex charges for (like GPU hardware transcoding for smooth playback) are completely free in Jellyfin.
sentences.zh: 免费本机硬件转码：Plex 收费的高级功能（例如用于流畅播放的 GPU 硬件转码）在 Jellyfin 中完全免费。

blocks.html_en: The Client Ecosystem Trade-Off: Corporate Standard vs. Scattered Community Apps

sentences.en: The Client Ecosystem Trade-Off: Corporate Standard vs. Scattered Community Apps
sentences.zh: 客户端生态系统权衡：企业标准与分散的社区应用程序

blocks.html_en: A realistic comparison between the two platforms also comes down to client availability and app stability:

sentences.en: A realistic comparison between the two platforms also comes down to client availability and app stability:
sentences.zh: 两个平台之间的实际比较还取决于客户端可用性和应用程序稳定性：

blocks.html_en: • Plex's Unbeatable Reach: Because Plex operates as a commercial company with dedicated app development teams, they maintain rock-solid native apps across virtually every Smart TV app store worldwide (LG webOS, Samsung Tizen, Apple TV, Roku, Fire TV, PlayStation, Xbox). Non-technical family members can download the app and start watching instantly without tinkering.

sentences.en: Plex's Unbeatable Reach: Because Plex operates as a commercial company with dedicated app development teams, they maintain rock-solid native apps across virtually every Smart TV app store worldwide (LG webOS, Samsung Tizen, Apple TV, Roku, Fire TV, PlayStation, Xbox).
sentences.zh: Plex 无与伦比的影响力：由于 Plex 作为一家商业公司运营，拥有专门的应用程序开发团队，因此他们在全球几乎每个智能电视应用程序商店（LG webOS、Samsung Tizen、Apple TV、Roku、Fire TV、PlayStation、Xbox）上维护坚如磐石的本机应用程序。

sentences.en: Non-technical family members can download the app and start watching instantly without tinkering.
sentences.zh: 非技术家庭成员可以下载该应用程序并立即开始观看，无需进行任何修改。

blocks.html_en: • Jellyfin's Scattered Client Reality: Jellyfin has client apps (such as Android TV, Swiftfin for iOS/Apple TV, Roku, and web interfaces), but because there isn't a single company managing them, client development is split across separate volunteer projects. As a result, apps aren't always rock-solid or feature-complete on every screen. A video file that plays smoothly on Android TV might crash on a Smart TV app wrapper, and native apps aren't always easily available on older TV app stores.

sentences.en: Jellyfin's Scattered Client Reality: Jellyfin has client apps (such as Android TV, Swiftfin for iOS/Apple TV, Roku, and web interfaces), but because there isn't a single company managing them, client development is split across separate volunteer projects.
sentences.zh: Jellyfin 分散的客户端现实：Jellyfin 拥有客户端应用程序（例如 Android TV、适用于 iOS/Apple TV 的 Swiftfin、Roku 和 Web 界面），但由于没有一家公司管理它们，因此客户端开发分散在单独的志愿者项目中。

sentences.en: As a result, apps aren't always rock-solid or feature-complete on every screen.
sentences.zh: 因此，应用程序并不总是在每个屏幕上都坚如磐石或功能完整。

sentences.en: A video file that plays smoothly on Android TV might crash on a Smart TV app wrapper, and native apps aren't always easily available on older TV app stores.
sentences.zh: 在 Android TV 上流畅播放的视频文件可能会在智能电视应用程序包装器上崩溃，并且本机应用程序并不总是很容易在旧版电视应用程序商店中提供。

blocks.html_en: My Advice for Newcomers: If you're getting into self-hosting today and building your first home server, I strongly suggest starting with Jellyfin. You avoid corporate bloat, mandatory cloud logins, and paywalled features right out of the box; just keep in mind which TV devices your household uses.

sentences.en: My Advice for Newcomers: If you're getting into self-hosting today and building your first home server, I strongly suggest starting with Jellyfin.
sentences.zh: 我对新人的建议：如果您今天要开始自托管并构建您的第一个家庭服务器，我强烈建议从 Jellyfin 开始。

sentences.en: You avoid corporate bloat, mandatory cloud logins, and paywalled features right out of the box; just keep in mind which TV devices your household uses.
sentences.zh: 您可以避免企业臃肿、强制云登录和开箱即用的付费功能；只需记住您的家庭使用哪些电视设备即可。

blocks.html_en: 5. The Open-Source Reality Check: Burnout, Governance, and the July 2026 Leadership Shakeup

sentences.en: The Open-Source Reality Check: Burnout, Governance, and the July 2026 Leadership Shakeup
sentences.zh: 开源现实检验：倦怠、治理和 2026 年 7 月的领导层改组

blocks.html_en: While Jellyfin represents the gold standard for true system control and personal freedom, open source isn't immune to crises of its own.

sentences.en: While Jellyfin represents the gold standard for true system control and personal freedom, open source isn't immune to crises of its own.
sentences.zh: 虽然 Jellyfin 代表了真正的系统控制和个人自由的黄金标准，但开源也不能免受其自身危机的影响。

blocks.html_en: In July 2026, the Jellyfin project experienced a massive leadership transition when several core maintainers, including Project Leader Joshua Boniface, Core Team Member Anthony Lavado, and Co-founder Andrew Rabert, announced their departures within days of each other.

sentences.en: In July 2026, the Jellyfin project experienced a massive leadership transition when several core maintainers, including Project Leader Joshua Boniface, Core Team Member Anthony Lavado, and Co-founder Andrew Rabert, announced their departures within days of each other.
sentences.zh: 2026 年 7 月，Jellyfin 项目经历了一次大规模的领导层换届，包括项目负责人 Joshua Boniface、核心团队成员 Anthony Lavado 和联合创始人 Andrew Rabert 在内的几位核心维护者在几天内相继宣布离职。

blocks.html_en: This high-profile exit highlighted a growing challenge across open-source software:

sentences.en: This high-profile exit highlighted a growing challenge across open-source software:
sentences.zh: 这次高调的退出凸显了开源软件面临的日益严峻的挑战：

blocks.html_en: • Maintainer Burnout: Supporting millions of users as an unpaid side project takes a heavy mental toll.

sentences.en: Maintainer Burnout: Supporting millions of users as an unpaid side project takes a heavy mental toll.
sentences.zh: 维护者倦怠：作为一个无偿的副业项目来支持数百万用户会造成严重的精神损失。

blocks.html_en: • Unrealistic Expectations: As homelab setups grow in popularity, volunteer developers increasingly face demanding end-user expectations, with users expecting unpaid volunteers to act like 24/7 paid support teams.

sentences.en: Unrealistic Expectations: As homelab setups grow in popularity, volunteer developers increasingly face demanding end-user expectations, with users expecting unpaid volunteers to act like 24/7 paid support teams.
sentences.zh: 不切实际的期望：随着家庭实验室设置的日益普及，志愿者开发人员越来越多地面临最终用户苛刻的期望，用户期望无偿志愿者像 24/7 付费支持团队一样行事。

blocks.html_en: • The AI-Generated PR Deluge: Modern maintainers face a flood of low-quality, AI-generated code submissions that clutter issue trackers, forcing maintainers to spend hours auditing unverified code instead of building features.

sentences.en: The AI-Generated PR Deluge: Modern maintainers face a flood of low-quality, AI-generated code submissions that clutter issue trackers, forcing maintainers to spend hours auditing unverified code instead of building features.
sentences.zh: AI 生成的 PR 洪流：现代维护人员面临着大量低质量、AI 生成的代码提交，这些代码提交使问题跟踪器变得混乱，迫使维护人员花费数小时审核未经验证的代码，而不是构建功能。

blocks.html_en: This news is an important reality check for homelabbers. When proprietary platforms like Plex restrict system freedom, we turn to open-source alternatives. But open source only survives if the community actively supports it.

sentences.en: This news is an important reality check for homelabbers.
sentences.zh: 这个消息对于家庭劳动者来说是一个重要的现实检验。

sentences.en: When proprietary platforms like Plex restrict system freedom, we turn to open-source alternatives.
sentences.zh: 当像 Plex 这样的专有平台限制系统自由时，我们会转向开源替代方案。

sentences.en: But open source only survives if the community actively supports it.
sentences.zh: 但开源只有在社区积极支持的情况下才能生存。

blocks.html_en: 6. Early Adopter Reality: Why I Still Use Plex (And Why I Run Jellyfin in Parallel)

sentences.en: Early Adopter Reality: Why I Still Use Plex (And Why I Run Jellyfin in Parallel)
sentences.zh: 早期采用者现实：为什么我仍然使用 Plex（以及为什么我并行运行 Jellyfin）

blocks.html_en: Looking at both sides of the coin reveals an important truth: self-hosting isn't an all-or-nothing choice.

sentences.en: Looking at both sides of the coin reveals an important truth: self-hosting isn't an all-or-nothing choice.
sentences.zh: 审视硬币的两面揭示了一个重要的事实：自托管并不是一个全有或全无的选择。

blocks.html_en: As an early adopter who built my setup around Plex over nearly a decade, I still use Plex every single day. For an existing setup that's running smoothly, the classic homelab rule applies: if it ain't broke, don't fix it.

sentences.en: As an early adopter who built my setup around Plex over nearly a decade, I still use Plex every single day.
sentences.zh: 作为一个围绕 Plex 建立了近十年设置的早期采用者，我仍然每天都使用 Plex。

sentences.en: For an existing setup that's running smoothly, the classic homelab rule applies: if it ain't broke, don't fix it.
sentences.zh: 对于运行顺利的现有设置，适用经典的家庭实验室规则：如果它没有损坏，就不要修复它。

blocks.html_en: I've set up Jellyfin myself alongside Plex in my homelab, and I run both servers in parallel. I follow Jellyfin's development closely, and one day, when the client app ecosystem and project maturity reach the right point, I may very well flick the switch and migrate 100%. But full migration isn't for me just yet; Plex remains my primary daily driver.

sentences.en: I've set up Jellyfin myself alongside Plex in my homelab, and I run both servers in parallel.
sentences.zh: 我自己在我的家庭实验室中与 Plex 一起设置了 Jellyfin，并且并行运行两台服务器。

sentences.en: I follow Jellyfin's development closely, and one day, when the client app ecosystem and project maturity reach the right point, I may very well flick the switch and migrate 100%.
sentences.zh: 我密切关注 Jellyfin 的开发，有一天，当客户端应用程序生态系统和项目成熟度达到正确的程度时，我很可能会轻按开关并 100% 迁移。

sentences.en: But full migration isn't for me just yet; Plex remains my primary daily driver.
sentences.zh: 但完全迁移还不适合我； Plex 仍然是我日常的主要司机。

blocks.html_en: This sentiment shift isn't unique to my homelab either. Even Linus Sebastian from Linus Tech Tips (LTT), historically one of Plex's biggest supporters online, slowly started complaining more about Plex's trajectory. On The WAN Show and LTT videos, Linus voiced growing frustration over cloud authentication lockouts, corporate pivots, and software bloat. Eventually, Linus Tech Tips went on to produce dedicated video content showcasing open-source alternatives like Jellyfin, showing how widespread the desire for self-hosted alternatives has become across tech culture.

sentences.en: This sentiment shift isn't unique to my homelab either.
sentences.zh: 这种情绪转变也不是我的家庭实验室所独有的。

sentences.en: Even Linus Sebastian from Linus Tech Tips (LTT), historically one of Plex's biggest supporters online, slowly started complaining more about Plex's trajectory.
sentences.zh: 甚至来自 Linus Tech Tips (LTT) 的 Linus Sebastian（历史上 Plex 最大的在线支持者之一）也慢慢开始对 Plex 的发展轨迹提出更多抱怨。

sentences.en: On The WAN Show and LTT videos, Linus voiced growing frustration over cloud authentication lockouts, corporate pivots, and software bloat.
sentences.zh: 在 WAN Show 和 LTT 视频中，Linus 表达了对云身份验证锁定、企业转型和软件膨胀日益增长的挫败感。

sentences.en: Eventually, Linus Tech Tips went on to produce dedicated video content showcasing open-source alternatives like Jellyfin, showing how widespread the desire for self-hosted alternatives has become across tech culture.
sentences.zh: 最终，Linus Tech Tips 继续制作了专门的视频内容，展示 Jellyfin 等开源替代方案，展示了对自托管替代方案的渴望在整个技术文化中的广泛程度。

blocks.html_en: When I bought my Lifetime Plex Pass back in February 2021 for $74.99 (~£54 GBP), the math made complete sense. Over the last 5.5 years (roughly 66 months of continuous daily use), that initial purchase works out to just ~£9.80 per year (or roughly 82p / $1.13 per month).

sentences.en: When I bought my Lifetime Plex Pass back in February 2021 for $74.99 (~£54 GBP), the math made complete sense.
sentences.zh: 当我在 2021 年 2 月以 74.99 美元（约 54 英镑）购买终身 Plex Pass 时，数学完全合理。

sentences.en: Over the last 5.5 years (roughly 66 months of continuous daily use), that initial purchase works out to just ~£9.80 per year (or roughly 82p / $1.13 per month).
sentences.zh: 在过去 5.5 年（大约连续日常使用 66 个月）中，首次购买每年仅需约 9.80 英镑（或每月大约 82 便士/1.13 美元）。

blocks.html_en: And chief among the tools justifying that purchase is Plexamp.

sentences.en: And chief among the tools justifying that purchase is Plexamp.
sentences.zh: Plexamp 是证明购买合理性的主要工具之一。

blocks.html_en: Plexamp is an absolute masterpiece of audio engineering. By self-hosting my music collection and streaming it via Plexamp, whether listening on mobile devices, home audio systems, or in the car via Android Auto and Apple CarPlay, I was able to completely stop paying for monthly subscriptions to Spotify and Amazon Music.

sentences.en: Plexamp is an absolute masterpiece of audio engineering.
sentences.zh: Plexamp 绝对是音频工程的杰作。

sentences.en: By self-hosting my music collection and streaming it via Plexamp, whether listening on mobile devices, home audio systems, or in the car via Android Auto and Apple CarPlay, I was able to completely stop paying for monthly subscriptions to Spotify and Amazon Music.
sentences.zh: 通过自行托管我的音乐收藏并通过 Plexamp 进行流式传输，无论是在移动设备、家庭音响系统上收听，还是在车内通过 Android Auto 和 Apple CarPlay 收听，我都能够完全停止支付 Spotify 和 Amazon Music 的每月订阅费用。

blocks.html_en: At ~£10.99/month ($11.99/month) for a commercial music streaming subscription, Plexamp paid for my Lifetime Pass within five months. Over 5.5 years, it's saved me well over £700 ($900) in recurring music streaming bills.

sentences.en: At ~£10.99/month ($11.99/month) for a commercial music streaming subscription, Plexamp paid for my Lifetime Pass within five months.
sentences.zh: 商业音乐流媒体订阅价格约为 10.99 英镑/月（11.99 美元/月），Plexamp 在五个月内支付了我的终身通行证。

sentences.en: Over 5.5 years, it's saved me well over £700 ($900) in recurring music streaming bills.
sentences.zh: 5.5 年来，它为我节省了超过 700 英镑（900 美元）的经常性音乐流媒体账单。

blocks.html_en: To Plex's credit, they've been remarkably good at adding value to the Lifetime Pass over the years, rolling out tools like Plexamp, intro skipping, hardware transcoding, and mobile downloads without asking early pass holders for another penny. Even if Plex eventually alters its terms down the road, I've undoubtedly gotten my money's worth out of that $75 purchase, and many other long-time users feel the exact same way.

sentences.en: To Plex's credit, they've been remarkably good at adding value to the Lifetime Pass over the years, rolling out tools like Plexamp, intro skipping, hardware transcoding, and mobile downloads without asking early pass holders for another penny.
sentences.zh: 值得赞扬的是，Plex 多年来一直非常擅长为终身通行证增值，推出了 Plexamp、介绍跳过、硬件转码和移动下载等工具，而无需向早期通行证持有者索要另一分钱。

sentences.en: Even if Plex eventually alters its terms down the road, I've undoubtedly gotten my money's worth out of that $75 purchase, and many other long-time users feel the exact same way.
sentences.zh: 即使 Plex 最终改变了它的条款，我无疑从这 75 美元的购买中得到了我的钱，而且许多其他长期用户也有同样的感觉。

blocks.html_en: But it raises a bigger question for the tech industry: Is a one-time lifetime license model actually sustainable long-term?

sentences.en: But it raises a bigger question for the tech industry: Is a one-time lifetime license model actually sustainable long-term?
sentences.zh: 但这给科技行业提出了一个更大的问题：一次性终身许可模式实际上是否可以长期可持续？

blocks.html_en: History shows how rare it is for tech companies to honor lifetime promises forever as cloud infrastructure costs and investor pressures grow. Major tech media coverage highlights how lifetime deals have been altered or revoked across the industry before:

sentences.en: History shows how rare it is for tech companies to honor lifetime promises forever as cloud infrastructure costs and investor pressures grow.
sentences.zh: 历史表明，随着云基础设施成本和投资者压力的增加，科技公司永远履行终生承诺是多么罕见。

sentences.en: Major tech media coverage highlights how lifetime deals have been altered or revoked across the industry before:
sentences.zh: 主要科技媒体的报道强调了之前整个行业的终身交易是如何改变或撤销的：

blocks.html_en: • TiVo: Sold popular "Lifetime Service" passes for DVRs, but as reported by The Verge, TiVo eventually phased out hardware lines, shut down guide data servers, and left legacy devices non-functional.

sentences.en: TiVo: Sold popular "Lifetime Service" passes for DVRs, but as reported by The Verge, TiVo eventually phased out hardware lines, shut down guide data servers, and left legacy devices non-functional.
sentences.zh: TiVo：出售流行的 DVR“终身服务”通行证，但据 The Verge 报道，TiVo 最终逐步淘汰了硬件产品线，关闭了指南数据服务器，并使旧设备无法运行。

blocks.html_en: • SiriusXM: Sold $500 "Lifetime" satellite radio subscriptions, but restricted device transfers and added hidden fees, resulting in a major nationwide class-action lawsuit detailed by Courthouse News.

sentences.en: SiriusXM: Sold $500 "Lifetime" satellite radio subscriptions, but restricted device transfers and added hidden fees, resulting in a major nationwide class-action lawsuit detailed by Courthouse News.
sentences.zh: SiriusXM：出售了 500 美元的“终身”卫星广播订阅，但限制设备转让并增加了隐藏费用，导致法院新闻详细介绍了一场重大的全国性集体诉讼。

blocks.html_en: • LogMeIn: Famously eliminated its popular "free for life" remote access tier with just seven days' notice to force users onto paid plans, as covered by TechCrunch.

sentences.en: LogMeIn: Famously eliminated its popular "free for life" remote access tier with just seven days' notice to force users onto paid plans, as covered by TechCrunch.
sentences.zh: LogMeIn：众所周知，仅提前 7 天通知就取消了其流行的“终身免费”远程访问套餐，迫使用户使用付费计划，正如 TechCrunch 报道的那样。

blocks.html_en: • Evernote & Malwarebytes: Malwarebytes grandfathered early lifetime keys before shifting strictly to annual plans, while Evernote stripped features out of legacy accounts to push users into new paid tiers, as reported by The Verge.

sentences.en: Evernote & Malwarebytes: Malwarebytes grandfathered early lifetime keys before shifting strictly to annual plans, while Evernote stripped features out of legacy accounts to push users into new paid tiers, as reported by The Verge.
sentences.zh: Evernote 和 Malwarebytes：据 The Verge 报道，Malwarebytes 在严格转向年度计划之前继承了早期生命周期密钥，而 Evernote 则从旧帐户中剥离了功能，以将用户推向新的付费级别。

blocks.html_en: The MRR Trap: Can Chasing Subscriptions Go On Forever?

sentences.en: The MRR Trap: Can Chasing Subscriptions Go On Forever?
sentences.zh: MRR 陷阱：追逐订阅能永远持续下去吗？

blocks.html_en: In today's software landscape, both solo developers and massive tech corporations are hyper-focused on securing Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR). To chase predictable recurring cash flow, companies increasingly rely on sneaky tactics: locking existing local features behind paywalls, adding unexpected subscription gates, and quietly changing terms of service to turn one-time buyers into permanent renters.

sentences.en: In today's software landscape, both solo developers and massive tech corporations are hyper-focused on securing Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR).
sentences.zh: 在当今的软件领域，独立开发人员和大型科技公司都非常注重确保每月经常性收入 (MRR)。

sentences.en: To chase predictable recurring cash flow, companies increasingly rely on sneaky tactics: locking existing local features behind paywalls, adding unexpected subscription gates, and quietly changing terms of service to turn one-time buyers into permanent renters.
sentences.zh: 为了追逐可预测的经常性现金流，公司越来越依赖偷偷摸摸的策略：将现有的本地功能锁定在付费墙后面，增加意想不到的订阅门，以及悄悄改变服务条款，将一次性买家变成永久租户。

blocks.html_en: It raises a critical question for both creators and consumers: Can this endless pursuit of MRR through sneaky tactics really go on forever? When software transitions from serving users to extracting monthly rent for hardware sitting in your own living room, it creates a deep trust deficit that drives users straight to open-source alternatives.

sentences.en: It raises a critical question for both creators and consumers: Can this endless pursuit of MRR through sneaky tactics really go on forever?
sentences.zh: 这给创作者和消费者都提出了一个关键问题：这种通过偷偷摸摸的策略无休止地追求MRR真的能永远持续下去吗？

sentences.en: When software transitions from serving users to extracting monthly rent for hardware sitting in your own living room, it creates a deep trust deficit that drives users straight to open-source alternatives.
sentences.zh: 当软件从为用户服务转变为为您客厅中的硬件收取月租时，就会产生严重的信任赤字，促使用户直接转向开源替代品。

blocks.html_en: When a company realizes one-time lifetime payments can't fund perpetual development and server hosting, they inevitably pivot toward ad networks, locked feature tiers, or price hikes, which is precisely what we're watching unfold with Plex today.

sentences.en: When a company realizes one-time lifetime payments can't fund perpetual development and server hosting, they inevitably pivot toward ad networks, locked feature tiers, or price hikes, which is precisely what we're watching unfold with Plex today.
sentences.zh: 当一家公司意识到一次性终身付款无法为永久开发和服务器托管提供资金时，他们不可避免地会转向广告网络、锁定功能层或价格上涨，这正是我们今天在 Plex 上看到的情况。

blocks.html_en: The Problem Today: Huge Price Increases & Eroding Value

sentences.en: The Problem Today: Huge Price Increases & Eroding Value
sentences.zh: 当今的问题：价格大幅上涨并侵蚀价值

blocks.html_en: However, what made financial sense in 2021 looks completely unrecognizable today. Historically, prospective homelabbers knew they could rely on Plex's regular annual sales events, such as Black Friday, Cyber Monday, February anniversary sales, Pro Days, or targeted email promo codes offering lifetime passes for $75 to $90.

sentences.en: However, what made financial sense in 2021 looks completely unrecognizable today.
sentences.zh: 然而，在 2021 年具有财务意义的东西今天看起来完全无法辨认。

sentences.en: Historically, prospective homelabbers knew they could rely on Plex's regular annual sales events, such as Black Friday, Cyber Monday, February anniversary sales, Pro Days, or targeted email promo codes offering lifetime passes for $75 to $90.
sentences.zh: 从历史上看，潜在的家庭实验室者知道他们可以依靠 Plex 的定期年度销售活动，例如黑色星期五、网络星期一、二月周年纪念销售、Pro Days 或有针对性的电子邮件促销代码，提供 75 至 90 美元的终身通行证。

blocks.html_en: For years, standard community advice for newcomers was simple: "Just hold off and wait for the Black Friday sale."

sentences.en: For years, standard community advice for newcomers was simple: "Just hold off and wait for the Black Friday sale."
sentences.zh: 多年来，社区对新人的标准建议很简单：“推迟并等待黑色星期五促销。”

blocks.html_en: Many users held off for months or years expecting those familiar discounts to return. But people were caught completely off-guard when Plex silently ended those sales events and instead hit the community with massive price hikes, covered across major tech media outlets like AppleInsider and official Plex Pass documentation:

sentences.en: Many users held off for months or years expecting those familiar discounts to return.
sentences.zh: 许多用户推迟了数月或数年，期待那些熟悉的折扣再次出现。

sentences.en: But people were caught completely off-guard when Plex silently ended those sales events and instead hit the community with massive price hikes, covered across major tech media outlets like AppleInsider and official Plex Pass documentation:
sentences.zh: 但当 Plex 悄然结束这些销售活动，转而以大幅涨价的方式冲击社区时，人们完全措手不及，AppleInsider 等主要科技媒体和 Plex Pass 官方文档都对此进行了报道：

blocks.html_en: • The Lifetime Pass: Historically $119.99 USD (and $75–$90 during sales), Plex first increased it to $249.99, before skyrocketing the Lifetime Plex Pass to an eye-watering $749.99 USD (~£595+ GBP) in July 2026.

sentences.en: The Lifetime Pass: Historically $119.99 USD (and $75–$90 during sales), Plex first increased it to $249.99, before skyrocketing the Lifetime Plex Pass to an eye-watering $749.99 USD (~£595+ GBP) in July 2026.
sentences.zh: 终身通行证：历史上为 119.99 美元（销售期间为 75-90 美元），Plex 首先将其提高到 249.99 美元，然后在 2026 年 7 月将终身 Plex 通行证飙升至令人眼花缭乱的 749.99 美元（约合 595 英镑+英镑）。

blocks.html_en: • New 5-Year Tier: Alongside the $750 lifetime price tag, Plex introduced a 5-Year Pass at $249.99 USD, effectively shifting what was once a true lifetime purchase into an expensive multi-year subscription cycle.

sentences.en: New 5-Year Tier: Alongside the $750 lifetime price tag, Plex introduced a 5-Year Pass at $249.99 USD, effectively shifting what was once a true lifetime purchase into an expensive multi-year subscription cycle.
sentences.zh: 新的 5 年套餐：除了 750 美元的终身价格标签外，Plex 还推出了 249.99 美元的 5 年通行证，有效地将曾经真正的终身购买转变为昂贵的多年订阅周期。

blocks.html_en: • The Monthly Alternative: Granted, you don't have to pay $750 up-front, as Plex still offers a monthly subscription at $6.99 / £6.99 per month.

sentences.en: The Monthly Alternative: Granted, you don't have to pay $750 up-front, as Plex still offers a monthly subscription at $6.99 / £6.99 per month.
sentences.zh: 包月替代方案：当然，您不必预先支付 750 美元，因为 Plex 仍然以每月 6.99 美元/6.99 英镑的价格提供包月订阅服务。

blocks.html_en: But that brings us to a fundamental question for self-hosters: Should you really have to pay a recurring monthly rent to a third-party company just to access and unlock features (like GPU hardware transcoding) on hardware you bought and own in your own home?

sentences.en: But that brings us to a fundamental question for self-hosters: Should you really have to pay a recurring monthly rent to a third-party company just to access and unlock features (like GPU hardware transcoding) on hardware you bought and own in your own home?
sentences.zh: 但这给我们带来了自我托管者的一个基本问题：您是否真的必须向第三方公司支付经常性的月租，才能访问和解锁您在自己家中购买和拥有的硬件上的功能（例如 GPU 硬件转码）？

blocks.html_en: Asking a new user to pay $750 up-front (or shell out $6.99 every single month indefinitely) for software that increasingly buries your personal media below ad-supported FAST channels, pushes commercial studio content, and locks your local server out during cloud authentication outages feels really hard to justify. When Jellyfin provides native GPU hardware transcoding, local multi-user auth, and full media streaming for $0, the reason to buy into Plex today just isn't there anymore for anyone starting fresh.

blocks.html_en: The debate between Plex and Jellyfin isn't about rigid tech dogma; it's about practical utility and freedom.

sentences.zh: Plex 和 Jellyfin 之间的争论并不是关于僵化的技术教条，而是关于僵化的技术教条。这是关于实用性和自由的。

blocks.html_en: • If you're starting out today: Go with Jellyfin. It gives you total control over your hardware without cloud gatekeeping or corporate ad clutter.

sentences.en: If you're starting out today: Go with Jellyfin.
sentences.zh: 如果您今天就开始：选择 Jellyfin。

blocks.html_en: However, we can't treat open-source projects as "free enterprise software." If you enjoy using Jellyfin or want to help the maintainers out, consider donating to the Jellyfin project to help support ongoing development and infrastructure.

content_html_zh:
从本周开始，我们将开始一个新系列，我想将其称为“星期四思考片”。 有些事情已经在我脑子里盘旋了一段时间，其中一些适合打印出来。

十多年来，Plex 一直是技术爱好者、DIY 爱好者和家庭实验室人员进入家庭媒体服务器的主要门户。 如果您不熟悉它，请将 Plex 视为您自己的私人、自托管 Netflix 或 Spotify。 您不需要昂贵的服务器机架或专用实验室来运行它：您可以轻松地将服务器软件安装在旧的备用笔记本电脑、未使用的台式电脑、简单的 NAS 上，甚至插入 Raspberry Pi 的外部硬盘驱动器上，保存您的个人电影、电视节目和音乐文件。 然后，Plex 会自动组织您的库，提取海报插图和电影详细信息，并在各种设备上无缝传输您的内容。 无论是在 LG webOS、三星 Tizen、Apple TV、Roku、Fire TV、iOS、Android 还是游戏机上，Plex 都让家里的任何人都可以非常轻松地登录并立即观看或收听他们的内容。

我自己的媒体流之旅实际上可以追溯到 2002 年末 / 2003 年，当时 XBMC（Xbox 媒体中心）运行在原始的改装 Xbox 控制台上。 当时，管理家庭媒体中心意味着要花费无数时间手动抓取艺术品、调整 NFO 文件以及搞乱 XML 设置。 但随着生活变得越来越忙碌，我根本没有时间再深入研究元数据方面的事情。 就在那时我转而使用 Plex，因为它开箱即用，不需要太多麻烦。

从 XBMC 转型后，我多年来一直依赖 Plex，在 2021 年 2 月大流行封锁期间的一次促销活动中，我以 74.99 美元（约合 54 英镑）的价格购买了 Plex 终身通行证。 被困在家里意味着要花更多的时间来完善我的家庭服务，那个时代成为我真正投入自我托管并扩展我的设置的转折点。 购买终身通行证感觉很正确：这是回馈项目的一种方式，该项目让我的家庭娱乐在多年免费使用后保持平稳运行，同时还解锁了 Plex Pass 功能，如硬件加速 GPU 转码、移动同步和介绍跳过。

但在过去几年中，围绕 Plex 的气氛发生了变化。 该软件经历了向企业蠕变（或“enshittification”）的缓慢转变，即公司慢慢地将企业交易和广告置于其原始核心用户之上。

作为一个长期的自我托管者，重视对我的硬件和设置的完全控制，看到这一变化是技术所有权方面令人大开眼界的一课。 它说明了为什么现在可能是时候转向 Jellyfin 以获得真正的系统自由了，同时还审视了长期支持社区替代方案所需的开源现实。

Plex 的核心业务模式已经发生转变。 最初，他们通过 Plex Pass 销售服务器软件。 如今，Plex 的主要目标是成为一个由主要工作室授权交易支持的集中式、广告支持的免费流媒体服务（与 PlutoTV 或 Tubi 竞争）。

公平地说，赞助内容和免费广告支持的流媒体电视 (FAST) 频道非常适合不托管自己媒体的休闲观众。 开箱即用的免费电影和直播频道为数百万用户带来了真正的便利。 然而，这些商业内容交易巧妙地将 Plex 的注意力从其最初的使命转移：无缝地为您自己的个人媒体提供服务。

今天在新设备上打开默认的 Plex 应用程序，您的个人服务器的库就会被隐藏。 相反，主屏幕上充斥着 Plex 自己的免费广告电影、赞助电视频道和商业广告。 虽然您可以在设置中隐藏这些内容，但自托管服务器默认推送外部广告支持的媒体这一事实表明了 Plex 的工程优先事项和公司收入现在所在的位置。

工作室压力和公司联盟：镇压和远程值守通行证

随着 Plex 与主要好莱坞电影公司合作获得免费流媒体内容许可，企业面临着清理其平台并符合商业流媒体标准的压力：

  • 商业主机打击：Plex 开始积极针对出售商业 Plex 服务器访问权限的帐户。 这升级为对主要云托管提供商的全面禁令（例如禁止整个 Hetzner 和 OVH 服务器 IP 范围）并清除与付费图书馆共享相关的帐户。 虽然停止非法服务器销售在法律上是有意义的，但禁止整个数据中心 IP 范围也会伤害那些只是在场外托管自己的私人服务器的普通家庭劳动者。
  • 订阅门控和远程观看通行证：为了满足内容合作伙伴的需求并通过户外观看者获利，Plex 引入了新的订阅摩擦，例如远程观看通行证（要求远程观看者或服务器所有者为户外播放付费），同时加强了终身 Plex Pass 权益在共享帐户中的应用方式。 家庭之间曾经无缝、开放的共享已逐渐被重组为封闭的企业层级。

2. 集中式身份验证陷阱

从系统控制的角度来看，Plex 存在一个单点故障，它破坏了拥有服务器的基本承诺：集中身份验证。

当您登录 Plex 服务器时，您不会根据主机数据库进行本地身份验证。 相反，您的应用程序会重定向到 Plex.tv 以通过其中央服务器登录，然后服务器会发回密钥以解锁对本地硬件的访问。

  • 中断风险：Status.Plex.tv 上跟踪的反复出现的身份验证中断，以及 BleepingComputer 和 How-To Geek 等高权威技术出版物报道的重大事件，都表明了这种设置的风险有多大。 当 Plex 的云基础设施出现停机或 DNS 中断时，全球数以千计的用户（包括付费 Plex Pass 订阅者）发现自己被锁在客厅里的本地服务器之外，无法传输本地文件，除非他们已经提前设置了离线网络设置。
  • 数据收集问题：每次用户打开您的 Plex 应用程序时，他们都会与 Plex 的中央服务器进行通信。 Plex 收集有关您观看的时间、您使用的设备以及您的观看列表中存在哪些项目的数据。

将此与 Jellyfin 进行比较：身份验证 100% 本地。 您的 Jellyfin 服务器是一个独立、独立的单元。 如果互联网连接中断或中央服务器发生故障，您的本地登录和本地流媒体完全不受影响。 您对自己的硬件保留完全的自由。

3. 范围蔓延和功能流失：改进的下载与弃用的功能

Plex 的功能开发路线图既有出色的技术修复，也有令人沮丧的功能删除：

  • 优点：移动下载终于可以工作了：值得赞扬的是，移动下载（同步）等核心功能多年来一直以错误和不可靠而闻名，但最近得到了专门的工程关注，现在在 iOS 和 Android 设备上工作得更好、更可靠。
  • 坏处：被弃用的功能（例如“一起观看”）：相反，用户经常会因心爱的功能被从其下删除而感到沮丧。 像“一起观看”这样的功能，在大流行期间成为与朋友和家人同步远程观看的重要生命线，但已被逐步淘汰或删除，让长期用户在他们依赖的工具消失时感到失望。
  • 社交附加功能：与此同时，Plex Discover（将用户引导至商业流媒体服务）和社交活动源等功能将工程人员的注意力吸引到参与度指标和数据收集上，进一步偏离了管理个人本地媒体的核心使命。

4. 开源回应：为什么 Jellyfin 获胜（以及我对新人的建议）

这种企业转变正是 Jellyfin（2018 年 Emby 闭源时从 Emby 分叉出来）赢得家庭实验室社区青睐的原因。

我在自己的家庭实验室中与 Plex 一起启动了 Jellyfin，以测试其性能、客户端应用程序和本地可靠性。 Jellyfin 建立在一个简单、令人耳目一新的前提之上：它是您的服务器、您的媒体和您的数据。

  • 无中央帐户：您不向中央服务器注册帐户。 用户帐户是在您的硬件上本地创建的。
  • 零跟踪：没有任何公司跟踪指标、广告行或强制发现功能。
  • 免费本机硬件转码：Plex 收费的高级功能（例如用于流畅播放的 GPU 硬件转码）在 Jellyfin 中完全免费。

客户端生态系统权衡：企业标准与分散的社区应用程序

两个平台之间的实际比较还取决于客户端可用性和应用程序稳定性：

  • Plex 无与伦比的影响力：由于 Plex 作为一家商业公司运营，拥有专门的应用程序开发团队，因此他们在全球几乎每个智能电视应用程序商店（LG webOS、Samsung Tizen、Apple TV、Roku、Fire TV、PlayStation、Xbox）上维护坚如磐石的本机应用程序。 非技术家庭成员可以下载该应用程序并立即开始观看，无需进行任何修改。
  • Jellyfin 分散的客户端现实：Jellyfin 拥有客户端应用程序（例如 Android TV、适用于 iOS/Apple TV 的 Swiftfin、Roku 和 Web 界面），但由于没有一家公司管理它们，因此客户端开发分散在单独的志愿者项目中。 因此，应用程序并不总是在每个屏幕上都坚如磐石或功能完整。 在 Android TV 上流畅播放的视频文件可能会在智能电视应用程序包装器上崩溃，并且本机应用程序并不总是很容易在旧版电视应用程序商店中提供。

我对新人的建议：如果您今天要开始自托管并构建您的第一个家庭服务器，我强烈建议从 Jellyfin 开始。 您可以避免企业臃肿、强制云登录和开箱即用的付费功能；只需记住您的家庭使用哪些电视设备即可。

5. 开源现实检验：倦怠、治理和 2026 年 7 月的领导层改组

虽然 Jellyfin 代表了真正的系统控制和个人自由的黄金标准，但开源也不能免受其自身危机的影响。

2026 年 7 月，Jellyfin 项目经历了一次大规模的领导层换届，包括项目负责人 Joshua Boniface、核心团队成员 Anthony Lavado 和联合创始人 Andrew Rabert 在内的几位核心维护者在几天内相继宣布离职。

这次高调的退出凸显了开源软件面临的日益严峻的挑战：

  1. 维护者倦怠：作为一个无偿的副业项目来支持数百万用户会造成严重的精神损失。
  2. 不切实际的期望：随着家庭实验室设置的日益普及，志愿者开发人员越来越多地面临最终用户苛刻的期望，用户期望无偿志愿者像 24/7 付费支持团队一样行事。
  3. AI 生成的 PR 洪流：现代维护人员面临着大量低质量、AI 生成的代码提交，这些代码提交使问题跟踪器变得混乱，迫使维护人员花费数小时审核未经验证的代码，而不是构建功能。

这个消息对于家庭劳动者来说是一个重要的现实检验。 当像 Plex 这样的专有平台限制系统自由时，我们会转向开源替代方案。 但开源只有在社区积极支持的情况下才能生存。

6. 早期采用者现实：为什么我仍然使用 Plex（以及为什么我并行运行 Jellyfin）

审视硬币的两面揭示了一个重要的事实：自托管并不是一个全有或全无的选择。

作为一个围绕 Plex 建立了近十年设置的早期采用者，我仍然每天都使用 Plex。 对于运行顺利的现有设置，适用经典的家庭实验室规则：如果它没有损坏，就不要修复它。

我自己在我的家庭实验室中与 Plex 一起设置了 Jellyfin，并且并行运行两台服务器。 我密切关注 Jellyfin 的开发，有一天，当客户端应用程序生态系统和项目成熟度达到正确的程度时，我很可能会轻按开关并 100% 迁移。 但完全迁移还不适合我； Plex 仍然是我日常的主要司机。

这种情绪转变也不是我的家庭实验室所独有的。 甚至来自 Linus Tech Tips (LTT) 的 Linus Sebastian（历史上 Plex 最大的在线支持者之一）也慢慢开始对 Plex 的发展轨迹提出更多抱怨。 在 WAN Show 和 LTT 视频中，Linus 表达了对云身份验证锁定、企业转型和软件膨胀日益增长的挫败感。 最终，Linus Tech Tips 继续制作了专门的视频内容，展示 Jellyfin 等开源替代方案，展示了对自托管替代方案的渴望在整个技术文化中的广泛程度。

当我在 2021 年 2 月以 74.99 美元（约 54 英镑）购买终身 Plex Pass 时，数学完全合理。 在过去 5.5 年（大约连续日常使用 66 个月）中，首次购买每年仅需约 9.80 英镑（或每月大约 82 便士/1.13 美元）。

Plexamp 是证明购买合理性的主要工具之一。

Plexamp 绝对是音频工程的杰作。 通过自行托管我的音乐收藏并通过 Plexamp 进行流式传输，无论是在移动设备、家庭音响系统上收听，还是在车内通过 Android Auto 和 Apple CarPlay 收听，我都能够完全停止支付 Spotify 和 Amazon Music 的每月订阅费用。

商业音乐流媒体订阅价格约为 10.99 英镑/月（11.99 美元/月），Plexamp 在五个月内支付了我的终身通行证。 5.5 年来，它为我节省了超过 700 英镑（900 美元）的经常性音乐流媒体账单。

值得赞扬的是，Plex 多年来一直非常擅长为终身通行证增值，推出了 Plexamp、介绍跳过、硬件转码和移动下载等工具，而无需向早期通行证持有者索要另一分钱。 即使 Plex 最终改变了它的条款，我无疑从这 75 美元的购买中得到了我的钱，而且许多其他长期用户也有同样的感觉。

但这给科技行业提出了一个更大的问题：一次性终身许可模式实际上是否可以长期可持续？

历史表明，随着云基础设施成本和投资者压力的增加，科技公司永远履行终生承诺是多么罕见。 主要科技媒体的报道强调了之前整个行业的终身交易是如何改变或撤销的：

  • TiVo：出售流行的 DVR“终身服务”通行证，但据 The Verge 报道，TiVo 最终逐步淘汰了硬件产品线，关闭了指南数据服务器，并使旧设备无法运行。
  • SiriusXM：出售了 500 美元的“终身”卫星广播订阅，但限制设备转让并增加了隐藏费用，导致法院新闻详细介绍了一场重大的全国性集体诉讼。
  • LogMeIn：众所周知，仅提前 7 天通知就取消了其流行的“终身免费”远程访问套餐，迫使用户使用付费计划，正如 TechCrunch 报道的那样。
  • Evernote 和 Malwarebytes：据 The Verge 报道，Malwarebytes 在严格转向年度计划之前继承了早期生命周期密钥，而 Evernote 则从旧帐户中剥离了功能，以将用户推向新的付费级别。

MRR 陷阱：追逐订阅能永远持续下去吗？

在当今的软件领域，独立开发人员和大型科技公司都非常注重确保每月经常性收入 (MRR)。 为了追逐可预测的经常性现金流，公司越来越依赖偷偷摸摸的策略：将现有的本地功能锁定在付费墙后面，增加意想不到的订阅门，以及悄悄改变服务条款，将一次性买家变成永久租户。

这给创作者和消费者都提出了一个关键问题：这种通过偷偷摸摸的策略无休止地追求MRR真的能永远持续下去吗？ 当软件从为用户服务转变为为您客厅中的硬件收取月租时，就会产生严重的信任赤字，促使用户直接转向开源替代品。

当一家公司意识到一次性终身付款无法为永久开发和服务器托管提供资金时，他们不可避免地会转向广告网络、锁定功能层或价格上涨，这正是我们今天在 Plex 上看到的情况。

当今的问题：价格大幅上涨并侵蚀价值

然而，在 2021 年具有财务意义的东西今天看起来完全无法辨认。 从历史上看，潜在的家庭实验室者知道他们可以依靠 Plex 的定期年度销售活动，例如黑色星期五、网络星期一、二月周年纪念销售、Pro Days 或有针对性的电子邮件促销代码，提供 75 至 90 美元的终身通行证。

多年来，社区对新人的标准建议很简单：“推迟并等待黑色星期五促销。”

许多用户推迟了数月或数年，期待那些熟悉的折扣再次出现。 但当 Plex 悄然结束这些销售活动，转而以大幅涨价的方式冲击社区时，人们完全措手不及，AppleInsider 等主要科技媒体和 Plex Pass 官方文档都对此进行了报道：

  • 终身通行证：历史上为 119.99 美元（销售期间为 75-90 美元），Plex 首先将其提高到 249.99 美元，然后在 2026 年 7 月将终身 Plex 通行证飙升至令人眼花缭乱的 749.99 美元（约合 595 英镑+英镑）。
  • 新的 5 年套餐：除了 750 美元的终身价格标签外，Plex 还推出了 249.99 美元的 5 年通行证，有效地将曾经真正的终身购买转变为昂贵的多年订阅周期。
  • 包月替代方案：当然，您不必预先支付 750 美元，因为 Plex 仍然以每月 6.99 美元/6.99 英镑的价格提供包月订阅服务。

但这给我们带来了自我托管者的一个基本问题：您是否真的必须向第三方公司支付经常性的月租，才能访问和解锁您在自己家中购买和拥有的硬件上的功能（例如 GPU 硬件转码）？

要求新用户预先支付 750 美元（或无限期地每月支付 6.99 美元）购买软件，这些软件越来越多地将您的个人媒体隐藏在广告支持的 FAST 频道下，推送商业工作室内容，并在云身份验证中断期间锁定您的本地服务器，这感觉真的很难证明是合理的。 当 Jellyfin 以 0 美元的价格提供原生 GPU 硬件转码、本地多用户身份验证和完整媒体流时，对于任何新手来说，今天购买 Plex 的理由就不再存在了。

务实的结论：完整的系统自由和积极的管理

Plex 和 Jellyfin 之间的争论并不是关于僵化的技术教条，而是关于僵化的技术教条。这是关于实用性和自由的。

  • 如果您今天就开始：选择 Jellyfin。 它使您可以完全控制您的硬件，而无需云把关或公司广告混乱。
  • 如果您是 Plex Pass 的长期用户：让 Plex 作为您的日常驱动程序，同时在您的家庭设置中与 Jellyfin 一起运行，这是零耻辱。 并行运行这两个工具可以让您享受当今的 Plexamp 等工具，同时定位您的基础设施，以便在 Jellyfin 达到完全客户端成熟度时完全切换。

但是，我们不能将开源项目视为“免费企业软件”。 如果我们希望像 Jellyfin 这样的替代品与商业工具一起蓬勃发展，技术爱好者、DIY 者和家庭劳动者必须积极做出贡献，无论是通过财政赞助、文档改进、代码审查还是设置健康的社区边界。 如果您喜欢使用 Jellyfin 或想要帮助维护人员，请考虑向 Jellyfin 项目捐款，以帮助支持正在进行的开发和基础设施。

归根结底，我真的很喜欢 Plex，而且要明确的是，Plex 并不邪恶。 他们在企业中走钢丝，试图为数百万不同的用户提供服务，同时尽可能合法地遵守主要好莱坞电影公司和版权法规。

管理如此规模的商业业务绝非易事，随着 Plex 的不断发展，它很可能会让我们所有人感到惊讶。 我已经使用 Plex 十年了，我将继续将它与 Jellyfin 一起用作我的主要日常驱动程序。

但像 Jellyfin 这样的开源项目仍然值得花朵。 拥有一个强大的、社区驱动的替代方案对于整体健康竞争至关重要：保持商业平台诚实，防止供应商完全锁定，并保证技术爱好者始终拥有获得完全系统自由的途径。
